titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert Carson to Herbert Asquith, 11 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert Carson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Herbert Henry Asquith (1852-1928) was Liberal Prime Minister of the United Kingdom from 1908 to 1916.

correspAction.placeName: 7 Waring Street, Belfast, Co. Antrim, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
copy
7 Waring Street
Belfast Sept 9<hirend=\'underline\">th</hirend=\'underline\"> 1916 Lord Stamfordham
Private Secretary to the King Buckingham Palace. London My Lord Re The Home Rule Bill and the Betrayal of
Ulster by the government. In the interests of His Majesty, The Crown, and the
British Empire I write you the following facts. The Home Rule Bill has not been submitted to the
Nation, Asquith has no mandate from the Parliamentary
voters of the United Kingdom to place theHome Rule Bill
on the Statue Book- The Parliament act was obtained by Asquith by false
representations, fraud & force and under threat to create
500 new Lords all Radicals or Home Rulers. The House
of Lords was to be reconstructed, this has not been done.
Asquith holds office by a compact with the Nationalists
by bribery and corruption and payment of £400 a year to
members out of British Ratepayers pockets. All their
voting since being paid for their services isillegal as the
members of Parliament were not re-elected-Asquith
dare not put his policy before the Electorate. The Home Rule Bill has been admitted by Govern—
—ment to be a bad Bill and unworkable. The Government
found it necessary to bring forward an amending Bill
and which has not yet been settled. On the outbreak of War Asquith called on the opposition
to back up the Government and show a solid & united front
to the Enemy, and he gave an undertaking that all
controversial matters & bills were to be hung up until after
the War without prejudice to either party.
Asquith now tries to place the condemned Home Rule Bill
on the Statute Book to please his rebel Nationalists supporters
and break faith with the Nation, and by so doing break up the
the British Nation and cause War in Irelandand which
will extend to England, if His Majesty signs this Bill
without first having it submitted to the Electors of the still
United Kingdom it will break up the British Empire
and may cause dethronement- Asquith has all
along been a traitor to his King + country, he + his cabinet
should have been taken to the tower yard and shot. Ulster people have not forgotten his dastardly plot to
murder the Loyal Ulster Protestants in their beds. General
Goughwas ordered to get into Ulster by forced marchers.
set the Province in a blaze + make the red blood flow
and drive the people to the coast towns where Churchill and
the battle-ships could destroy them + the towns + ruin Ulster In this way Ulster Protestants Loyaltywas to be rewarded-
theGerman Emperorcould not be plotting a more
diabolical outrage than this- And now Lord Kitchener calls
for volunteers from Ulster to fight for England + the Flag. Ulster
has responded to the call. Asquith would again betray Ulster. The Irish Nationalist volunteers 300.000 strong, how many of
them have volunteered to fight for old England at this crisis. They
shout that England's difficulty is Ireland opportunity I know
Ireland well for many years. always the Irish roman Catholics
have been rebels, and the sooner Lord Kitchener rounds them
up and sends them to the front the better it will be for the
United Kingdom. Should Asquith put this condemned
Home Rule Bill before His Majesty let him consider
well before signing it.
I am my Lord
yours faithfully
Robert Carson
